title: Duck Game

notes:
Game requires some tweaks to get running.

After trying to run the game once to create the proton wine prefix, run "protontricks 312530 dotnet40" to install .NET 4.0 (if it asks to repair or remove, select repair) and "protontricks 312530 dinput" to install native DInput. Afer that the game will run.

Game runs great. Mods and custom maps download automatically and work, although I did not do a lot of testing with mods. Controller just works. Online works great. No graphics glitches, which I was getting with the Mono/XnaToFna fix people were suggesting below.
title: Duck Game

title: Duck Game

notes:
I came across multiple issues getting things working, it works 100% now but it required some effort, here are some remedies if things don't go smoothly for other players:

TLDR:
Use Proton 4.2-3, Run Duck Game to generate the prefix
Run `protontricks 312530 dotnet40`
and `protontricks 312530 dinput`.

 -- If problems occur, put `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` for launch options and read the generated `~/steam-312530.log`, and debug from there.

 -- If during `protontricks 312530 dotnet40`, the installer complains that you already have "Same or higher version of .NET Framework 4 has already been installed on this computer", then a few things need to be changed:

    1) Run `protontricks --gui` -> Duck Game -> Select default wine prefix -> Run regedit.    Search for anything that references .NET Framework (using View -> Find...)    There should be two places (one for 32 bit, one for 64 bit) where the structure goes somewhere along the lines of (... -> .NET Framework  ->  policies  ->  {v1..., v2..., v3..., v4...}).  Delete every v1,v2,v3,v4, but not the parent key folders.  There are some other keys referencing .NET framework versions that I deleted anyways, not sure if it was necessary.  **Do not delete anything referencing "Xna", "Xna.Framework", etc.**
    2a)  Go to the wine prefix on disk (by default `~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/312530/pfx`), and into the `drive_c/windows/Microsoft.NET` folder.
    2b) In `Framework` delete the four folders in there {v1..., v2..., v3..., v4...}
    2c) Repeat ^ for `Framework64`.
    3) Retry `protontricks 312530 dotnet40`, it should then hopefully succeed.

 -- If you get an error referencing "System.IO.FileNotFoundException: Could not load file or assembly 'Microsoft.Xna.Framework.dll' or one of its dependencies. Module not found."
    1)  Check a few lines above, there may be a message referencing `libFAudio.so.0`,  along the lines of "0009:err:module:load_builtin_dll failed to load .so lib for builtin L"X3DAudio1_7.dll": libFAudio.so.0: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory".
    2) If there is no such message, this fix will not apply. If there is, continue reading.
    3) I believe this error is caused by Proton (currently) not successfully shipping the libFAudio library, which is necessary for wine 4.2's XAudio implementation.  To fix this we need to install libFAudio, which isn't in many official package distributions at the current moment.  For Ubuntu, the following forum described a successful method to installing libFAudio: https://forum.winehq.org/viewtopic.php?t=32061
    3a) `sudo add-apt-repository ppa:cybermax-dexter/sdl2-backport`
    3c) `sudo apt install libfaudio`
    4) Now the prior error message should go away, and XNA should load successfully.

  After performing those steps,  Duck Game worked absolutely flawlessly, custom content and textures, online multiplayer, etc. worked 100%.  Hopefully these fixes could help players in the meantime until Proton evolves sufficiently where these hoops won't be necessary.
  Addendum:  For the .NET Framework "cleaning", the reason I deleted all four stub proton versions, instead of just v4, was that when I did initially delete just the v4 references, (and installing dotnet40), I got a startup error saying "CLR error: 80004005", which upon googling it, said that this error occurs when multiple .NET versions are installed simultaneously and incompatibility.  Thus I deleted all four versions and the game worked fine.
  Addendum #2:  The formatting on this site reeaaallllly sucks.   Because all the responses are manually checked anyway, couldn't regular newline characters be allowed for readibility purposes?

title: Duck Game

notes: Everything was tested and works. You have to "protontricks 312530 dotnet40" and "protontricks 312530 dinput". If you encounter the error about .NET 4.0 already being installed I had success with using Microsofts .NET cleanup tool, it's a lot more consistent and easier than fucking around yourself with the registry: https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/astebner/2008/08/28/net-framework-cleanup-tool-users-guide/
title: Duck Game

notes: To run this game you need to run this command: protontricks 312530 -q dotnet40 dinput --force. (avoid the last dot) This command avoids UI, thats the q parameter, and then ignores another possible dotnet install, and overwrites it, this avoids touching registry, after that like most of the games, you need to put in windowed to avoid crash when minimize or lose focus, and you can fake fullscreen/borderless using Kwin (Plasma) Window application rules, forcing size, no decorations/border. Keep in mind that if you test some patches or protontricks before, may you need to uninstall and install the game to get a clean start, I did that.
title: Duck Game

notes: Needed to install protontricks and run "protontricks 312530 -q dotnet40 dinput --force". After that, works perfectly, with performance similar to native.

title: Duck Game

notes:
Technically this doesn't work with Proton, but you can still get it running within Steam (without making it a non Steam game).  None of the protontricks stuff worked for me.
What you'll have to do is install the FNA patches for Duck Game (https://github.com/0x0ade/DuckGame-Linux) and then in the game's launch options, put "mono DuckGame.exe && echo %command%" (without the quotes). This basically bypasses Proton, but you can still play the game and it works with achievements and such. Only downsides are no mods and it doesn't seem like your saves sync from Windows.
title: Duck Game

notes: Run the game one time to create the wineprefix. Make sure you have Protontricks and then type `protontricks --gui`, select Duck Game, hit ok, click "Select the default wineprefix" > "Run uninstaller" and then uninstall Wine Mono. Then exit protontricks and run `protontricks 312530 dotnet40 dinput`. Runs smooth as butter after that.
title: Duck Game

notes:
After tweaks this works perfectly.

After creating the wineprefix by launching the game once, first run
"protontricks 312530 --gui"
Then select the default prefix and navigate to the uninstaller,  and then remove mono. 

Afterwards run
"protontricks 312530 dotnet40 dinput"

Once this process is complete, the game ran flawlessly, gamepads, multiplayer and mods all seem to behave as on windows.
title: Duck Game

notes:
Okay, first of all - all dotnet40 tweaks didn't work and it keeps crashing
So i decided to try a patch, that someone made (XNA to FNA). It was good but very outdated. I found that someone updated packages and game runs smoothly and without crashes every 10 min.
You can find new version here: https://github.com/xicronic/DuckGameLinux10-08-2018
and guide how to install that patch is here: https://github.com/0x0ade/DuckGame-Linux
